conditionDescriptions: Severe chronic kidney disease with eGFR <30 ml/min per 1.73 m²
contraindications.clinicalRationale: SGLT2 inhibitor trials, including VERTIS CV for ertugliflozin, required minimum eGFR ≥30 ml/min per 1.73 m² for enrollment, indicating safety and efficacy are not established below this threshold
contraindications.riskDescription: Lack of safety and efficacy data in severe CKD
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Other glucose-lowering agents appropriate for severe CKD
contraindications.sourceSnippet: VERTIS-CV: Ertugliflozin 5/15 mg once daily, 8246 participants, eGFR ≥30. The VERTIS CV trial enrolled 8246 patients with T2D and ASCVD (22% of participants had eGFR < 60 ml/min per 1.73 m2)

warnings.warningText: Volume depletion has been identified as a key adverse event in ertugliflozin clinical trials
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients with type 2 diabetes receiving ertugliflozin
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor for signs and symptoms of volume depletion, especially in patients at higher risk
